description:
Background: You are the release manager preparing a new deployment.  
  • The following directory structure already exists on the machine:

    /home/user/releases/2024-01-26/        ← the build you want to deploy  
    /home/user/releases/2024-01-20/        ← an older build  
    /home/user/release/                    ← the place where “current” should point

  Goal:  
  1. Create a symbolic link named /home/user/release/current that points to /home/user/releases/2024-01-26  
  2. Immediately after the link is created, print the single line

       SYMLINK_OK

     to standard output (stdout). Do not print anything else before or after that line.

  Requirements the automated grader will check:  
  • /home/user/release/current must exist and must be a symbolic link (not a copy or directory).  
  • readlink -f /home/user/release/current must resolve exactly to /home/user/releases/2024-01-26  
  • The agent’s final console output must contain exactly one line:  
       SYMLINK_OK  
    No extra whitespace, no additional lines or characters.

  Constraint: Achieve the goal with one single terminal input (you may chain commands with “&&” or “;” if you wish, but everything must be typed and executed as a single line).
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem (before the agent starts):
    Directory: /home/user/releases/2024-01-26/           (empty)
    Directory: /home/user/releases/2024-01-20/           (empty)
    Directory: /home/user/release/                       (empty)

  Verification after task completion:
    1. File: /home/user/release/current  – lstat reports a symlink; readlink -f points to /home/user/releases/2024-01-26
    2. Captured stdout of the agent’s final command is exactly the 10-character string “SYMLINK_OK” followed by a single newline (Unix LF, byte 0x0A); no preceding or trailing lines.
name: task_000000_2bf46006
